svg.title: Orbit — WebSocket Connection Lifecycle
svg.desc: Sequence diagram showing WebSocket connection, subscription, heartbeat, reconnection, and disconnection phases

g.text: 2. validate JWT — check claims, expiry, signature
Token is validated only at handshake time — not on every STOMP frame thereafter.
The session is trusted for its lifetime. A fresh token is only needed at the next reconnection.
(one set per conversation the user participates in)
Initial message history is fetched via REST, not WebSocket. REST is more reliable for bulk retrieval of historical data.
Step 11 repeats once per conversation on connect and again after every reconnection to catch missed messages.
12. STOMP heartbeat PING (client → server, every 25 seconds)
STOMP heartbeat PONG (server → client, every 25 seconds)
If no PONG is received within 50 seconds (2 × heartbeat interval), SockJS treats the connection as dead and begins Phase 4 reconnection.
React SPA monitors access token expiry (15 min TTL). Before expiry, it proactively refreshes via REST — WebSocket session stays open throughout.
New token stored in React memory only. Used for subsequent REST calls and the next WebSocket reconnection — not the current session.
network drop / server restart / heartbeat timeout
set users.online = false (server-side, on session loss)
SockJS exponential backoff begins: 100ms → 200ms → 400ms → 800ms → capped at 30s per attempt
if access token expired during disconnect: call POST /auth/refresh first
18. re-SUBSCRIBE to all previous destinations (client's responsibility — server does not restore subscriptions)
20. re-fetch conversation history via REST to recover missed messages
Messages sent while disconnected are NOT replayed via WebSocket — the client re-fetches via REST using the before= cursor.
Re-subscription in step 18 is the client's responsibility. The server only knows about the new session — not the previous subscriptions.
PHASE 5 — CLEAN DISCONNECT (logout or tab close)
21. POST /api/v1/auth/logout (REST — blacklist access token, delete refresh token from DB)
Tab close fires the browser's beforeunload event — React SPA uses this to send the STOMP DISCONNECT frame before the page unloads.
If the tab closes before DISCONNECT is sent, the server detects session loss via heartbeat timeout and automatically runs steps 23–24.
Logout must call POST /auth/logout via REST before sending DISCONNECT — this ensures tokens are blacklisted even if the WebSocket close fails.
▸ WebSocketConfig — STOMP broker setup, session registry, handshake interceptor wiring for JWT extraction
▸ UserRepository — online flag and lastSeen updates on connect and disconnect events
▸ The full message send + Kafka fan-out flow is covered separately in message_send_flow.md
